reasoning: The Lean statement negates "every element of ZMod n has a two-sided multiplicative inverse with respect to 1," which is logically equivalent to "ZMod n is not a group under multiplication." Associativity of multiplication is automatic, and the multiplicative identity on ZMod n must be 1 (since any identity e satisfies e·1 = 1, forcing e = 1), so specifying 1 as the identity does not narrow scope. The only way ZMod n could fail to be a group under multiplication is by failing the inverse axiom, and that is precisely what the Lean statement negates. Hence the two statements agree under any standard mathematical reading.
